figures.caption: Fig. 5: Illustration of the interactions of the user with LLM module in the validation loop of the experiments.

Fig. 1: Overview of the system. The LLM is queried by a new scenario or the user (blue arrows). It proposes modifications that must be validated by the user (green arrows). The new weights are applied in the MPPI (red arrows). The user may continue sending queries as the vehicles navigates (gray arrow).

Fig. 6: Snapshots of a ramp-merge scenario where the vehicle faces a non-cooperative vehicle, using the set of weights derived from the description s1 (a), those derived from s2 (b), and a combination of s1 and s2 depending on the scenario state (c). The plots follow the same representation as Fig. 4.

Fig. 2: Prompt used in our LLM module. The [brackets] symbolize placeholders that are substituted by the weights descriptions and behavior rules in Fig. 3, the current weights value, the scenario description and the user query.
Fig. 3: Descriptions of the cost terms and behavior rules included in the LLM prompt.
TABLE II: Parameters resulting from the queries of the experiments in Sec. V-A (top), Sec. V-B (middle) and Sec. V-C (bottom).
